steps.title: Determine the side length from the perimeter constraint
steps.content:
The trapezoid is formed by three congruent equilateral triangles: two pointing upward (shaded) at the bottom corners, and one pointing downward (unshaded) in the middle. Let each equilateral triangle have side length $s$.

The trapezoid's perimeter consists of:
  • Bottom edge: $2s$ (spanning the bases of both upward triangles)
  • Left edge: $s$ (left side of the left triangle)
  • Top edge: $s$ (the span between the two upward peaks)
  • Right edge: $s$ (right side of the right triangle)
Therefore:
$$5s = 20$$
$$s = 4 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Calculate the area of one equilateral triangle
steps.content:
The area formula for an equilateral triangle with side length $s$ is:
$$A = \frac{s^2\sqrt{3}}{4}$$
Substituting $s = 4$:
$$A = \frac{16\sqrt{3}}{4} = 4\sqrt{3} \text{ cm}^2$$

steps.title: Find the combined area of the two shaded regions
steps.content:
The two shaded triangles (left and \right) are congruent equilateral triangles. Their combined area is:
$$2 \times 4\sqrt{3} = 8\sqrt{3} \text{ cm}^2$$
C is correct.
